frameworks.name: Spectral Submanifolds (SSMs)
frameworks.one_line: Low-dimensional attracting invariant manifolds tangent to fixed-point eigenspaces.

frameworks.name: Recurrent Neural Networks
frameworks.one_line: High-dimensional RNN dynamics reduced via nonlinear invariant manifold theory.

frameworks.name: Attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Line and ring attractors used to explain decision-making and working memory.

core_question: Can data-driven nonlinear manifold techniques recover the core low-dimensional dynamics underlying high-dimensional RNNs used in neuroscience?
key_assumptions: RNN activity is organized around fixed points with attracting invariant manifolds
Spectral submanifolds tangent to eigenspaces capture essential dynamics
Dimensionality reduction by orders of magnitude is feasible without loss of core behavior
manipulations: Choice of RNN architectures from contemporary neuroscience tasks
Identification of SSMs around different fixed points
key_findings: SSMs identified data-drivenly serve as nonlinear reduced models of full RNNs
SSM-reduced models cut RNN dimensionality by orders of magnitude
Mathematically precise definitions of line and ring attractors are obtained via SSM reduction
interpretations: Nonlinear dynamical systems theory clarifies the geometric structure underlying RNN computation
Intuitive attractor concepts in neuroscience can be formalized through invariant manifold reductions
